free_text: Cross sections for the production of helium and long-
living radioactive isotopes by protons and deuterons
Targets were high purity Al, Ti, V, Fe, Ni and Cu
and also 316l type alloy with 65.1% Fe, 17.7% Cr, 12.3%
The targets were soldered to a copper bar mounted in a
vacuum chamber. The primary particle energies were
8.16 And 24 MeV for protons and 9 and 14 MeV for
deuteron particles. The beam current was determined
from the deposited heating power in the copper bar.
the error of the determination of heating power was 5%
and the energy error was 7%, from these errors the
estimated total uncertainty in the particle dose was
The helium content of the target after irr.
was determined by helium mass spectrometry, only He-4
was detected. Parts of the targets were also analyzed
Correction was made for the surface losses, for the
recoil loss of the alpha particles from the near
surface of the target foils and for the recoil
implantation of alpha particles from the sample holder.
the correction was less than 10% in all cases.
The energy spread in the target was typically
The contributions of the total error are
- Error of total particle dose absorbed     10%
data were taken from table 2. Of proc. Of int.
